content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
PARRINELLO, MUELLER & NAYLOR
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

SACRAMENTO                                    SAN FRANCISCO
770 L STREET, SUITE 800             591 REDWOOD HIGHWAY, #4000              650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814         MILL VALLEY, CALIFORNIA 94941            SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752            TELEPHONE (415) 389-6800               TELEPHONE (415) 389-6800


                                        July 8, 1992


Ms. Linda Cassidy
Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Analysis & Assistance
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California  95812-0807

     Re:  Tosco Refining Company's Third Quarter 1991
          Lobbyist Employer Report

Dear Linda:

     This letter requests written confirmation of advice you
provided to me in person last week and over the telephone earlier
this week.  (Gov. Code § 83116 and California Code Regs.
§ 18239.)  We have provided you with all facts material to
answering our question, and would therefore appreciate receiving
formal advice.  We are the authorized representative of Tosco
Refining Company and request this advice on its behalf.

                              Facts

     Tosco recently hosted a large dinner and dancing event to
reward employees for their hard work on a particular company
project.  The event cost approximately $30,000; the cost per
person was $70.  (This figure includes dinner, drinks, a band and
other entertainment, hotel rental, transportation costs, etc.)
An employee invited two state officials to the event, one of whom
represents the district where the company is located.  The event
would have been held had they not attended.  No arrangements for
the event were modified in consideration of their expected
attendance.

     On its Form 635 filed for that quarter, Tosco disclosed that
it had made activity expenses to each of the state officials in
the amount of $70, for a total activity expense of $140.  The
Secretary of State sent back the Form 635, indicating that the
company had not properly disclosed the total costs of the
activity expense event.  (See May 12 1992, letter enclosed).


Ms. Linda Cassidy
July 8, 1992
Page 2

                                    Analysis

     Lobbyist employers must report any payments they make which
benefit state officials, along with the total cost of any event
at which the legislators receive such payments. (Gov. Code
§ 86116.) In other words, if a company pays for dinner for an
employee (who is not a registered lobbyist) and two state
officials at $75 total and $25 per person, the lobbyist employer
must report both that the state officials received $25 activity
expenses and that the total cost of the event was $75. In order
to complete the summary section of Form 635, the lobbyist
employer must list the total costs of all such events.

     You indicated that Tosco need not list $30,000 as the total
cost of the event, because the event itself was not related to
the company's lobbying interests. Instead, the company invited
state officials to, and made activity expenses at, an event
unrelated to any legislative or political goals. In this case,
you told me that the "total cost of the activity expense event
was $140 ($70 times 2). Given the event size and the fact that
the company bore the cost for a nonlegislative reason, we agree
with your conclusion and unless told otherwise by you will advise
Tosco not to amend their Form 635. Your conclusion is identical
to the instructions provided in FPPC Lobbying Newsletter,
vol. 12, no. 1 (Jan. 1990) at p. 7 (copy enclosed).

     Please call me immediately if this letter does not
accurately reflect your advice.

                                    Sincerely,

                                    [signature]
                                    James R. Sutton

JRS/sfr

Enclosures

cc:  Barbara Smith, Secretary of State's Office
     Ann Farner, Tosco
     Jill Brumleve, Tosco

5714.01

sections.question: Whether Tosco Refining Company must report the total cost of a $30,000 employee reward dinner event ($70 per person) on its Form 635 Lobbyist Employer Report when two state officials were invited and received $70 in activity expenses each, or whether only the $140 total activity expense ($70 × 2) should be reported since the event was unrelated to the company's lobbying interests.

sections.question_synthetic: Must a lobbyist employer report the full cost of an employee appreciation event on Form 635 when state officials attend, or only the per-person cost multiplied by the number of officials attending, when the event is unrelated to lobbying activities?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney seeking formal written confirmation of prior informal FPPC advice. The letter describes facts, references prior advice, and requests confirmation but does not contain a separate 'Question' or 'Conclusion' section with verbatim formal responses. The attorney summarizes the FPPC's prior informal position within the body of the letter.

embedding.summary: A law firm requests written confirmation from FPPC of prior informal advice regarding whether Tosco Refining Company should report the total $30,000 cost of an employee dinner event on its Form 635, or only the $140 in activity expenses provided to two attending state officials, given the event was unrelated to lobbying.
